reviewer_question: Is the shard-002 comparator package publishable even though only one AVID direct join and one bounded AIID/OECD overlap pilot exist?
compact_reviewer_safe_answer: Yes, but only as an interoperability-asymmetry and claim-ceiling package. The current evidence supports a reviewer-safe demonstration that six external source families occupy five different publication roles with sharply different exactness ceilings; it does not support a general external-validation, completeness, or replicated-interoperability claim.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Keep the package at role heterogeneity, bounded overlap, and source-specific claim ceilings; do not widen it into completeness, validation-rate, or denominator-performance rhetoric.
paper_thesis_use: Compact publishability-now answer for reviewer rebuttal, cover-letter positioning, thesis defense notes, and methods/results prose.
visualization_hook: Table 6A reviewer-warning card; comparator role-ladder inset; hostile-reviewer answer panel.
next_smallest_publishability_move: If comparator work reopens substantively, spend it on one threshold-changing event rather than more packaging-only narration.

reviewer_question: Why are AIID and OECD not treated as one generic incident-database success story once each has one exact overlap pilot?
compact_reviewer_safe_answer: Because the two rows answer different questions and remain bounded in different ways. AIID is payload-stronger but snapshot-lag bounded; OECD is discovery-stronger but rights/reuse bounded. One exact overlap for each row proves a narrow local same-event pilot, not common mechanics, open reuse, or interchangeable comparator status.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Keep AIID and OECD as two distinct metadata-first subclasses with one bounded exact overlap each; do not flatten them into one readiness badge or imply bulk/API/archive equivalence.
paper_thesis_use: Compact middle-tier comparator answer for rebuttals, legend text, thesis discussion, and methods appendix.
visualization_hook: AIID/OECD subclass-contrast inset; tooltip pair; one-pilot-per-row badge.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Change this answer only if a second exact overlap or materially stronger route mechanics moves one row's ceiling.

reviewer_question: Why not describe the security side as already having two direct comparator joins because AVID and MITRE ATLAS both expose exact identifiers?
compact_reviewer_safe_answer: Because identifier visibility is not the same as same-case identity. AVID currently has one canonical direct local join tied to an exact security row, while MITRE ATLAS remains a technique/context graph bridge with zero defensible direct local case-study IDs. Product overlap, generalized case context, and exact technique IDs are useful evidence, but they still do not constitute a second direct join.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Keep AVID at one canonical direct join and ATLAS at technique-context-only unless one exact ATLAS case-study ID becomes defensible for a seeded local row.
paper_thesis_use: Compact exact-versus-analogue answer for reviewer memos, security methods prose, and thesis interoperability notes.
visualization_hook: Security exactness split panel; direct-vs-placeholder badge; ATLAS technique-context gap card.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Prefer one real AVID or ATLAS threshold event over any wording refresh about security comparators.

reviewer_question: Why keep MIT AI Risk and CSET in Table 6A when they still have zero populated local slots?
compact_reviewer_safe_answer: Because they still do useful but narrower work: MIT AI Risk supplies a visible concept/taxonomy bridge with public dataset metadata and named anchors, while CSET supplies a bounded manual harm-language overlay. Their value is precisely that they show concept-layer and manual-overlay roles without pretending row-level interoperability.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Keep both rows below row-level interoperability, anonymous export proof, or machine-ingest rhetoric; use them only as concept/manual overlays unless a clearly stronger threshold lands.
paper_thesis_use: Compact overlay-role answer for methods sections, reviewer responses, and thesis limitations language.
visualization_hook: Concept-overlay badge; manual-overlay badge; comparator role-ladder legend.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Do not reopen MIT AI Risk or CSET unless a concrete manuscript need or a true threshold event justifies it.

reviewer_question: Does the stronger comparator packaging justify a new shared empirical claim or higher LIMEN evidence tiers?
compact_reviewer_safe_answer: No. The current gain is package governance, not empirical promotion. The crosswalk stack is stronger, easier to cite, and less drift-prone, but no packaging-only step upgrades LIMEN evidence tiers, creates replicated overlap, proves same-event identity beyond the exact bounded rows, or warrants a new project-level shared empirical claim.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Keep comparator improvements at methods/results-control status until a threshold-changing event materially alters occupancy or exactness.
paper_thesis_use: Compact claim-governance answer for reviewer rebuttal, thesis claim-support maps, and manuscript caution notes.
visualization_hook: No-new-shared-claim badge; occupancy-authority note; claim-ceiling sidecar.
next_smallest_publishability_move: Only revisit shared-claim posture after a real occupancy-changing event such as a second AVID join, second AIID/OECD overlap, or first direct ATLAS case-study ID.

reviewer_question: What is the next honest comparator move if a reviewer asks for one stronger piece of evidence rather than better framing?
compact_reviewer_safe_answer: One threshold-changing event only: second canonical AVID direct join first, first defensible direct MITRE ATLAS case-study ID second, then a second exact AIID or OECD overlap only after those higher-yield security thresholds. That order changes publishability more than more route prose, more concept-layer packaging, or more manual-overlay commentary.
claim_ceiling_preserved: Keep route order explicit and threshold-based; do not reopen already-resolved first-pilot or visible-anchor work as if it were the main frontier.

claim_ceiling_preserved: Keep visible aggregate same-event-family coverage explicit, but do not narrate it as a second canonical AIID/OECD pilot, denominator growth, or unrestricted interoperability.
